records.ety: [OF. cors (sometimes written corps), F. corps, L. corpus; akin to AS. hrif womb. See Midriff, and cf. Corse, Corselet, Corps, Cuerpo.]

defs: A human body in general, whether living or dead; -- sometimes contemptuously.
notes: ☞ Formerly written (after the French form) corps. See Corps, n., 1.

defs: The dead body of a human being; -- used also Fig.
quotes: He touched the dead corpse of Public Credit, and it sprung upon its feet.
